fr.recommendations: Enhancing the main building's emergency response attributes could involve ensuring that the single access point and driveway remain unobstructed and clearly marked, which may assist emergency personnel in quickly locating and accessing the property. The implementation of clear signage can improve visibility and guide responders to appropriate entry routes. Additionally, evaluating the perimeter fencing for designated entry sections could facilitate swift responder entry and exit. If possible, consideration of an access point to the rear of the property may enhance emergency operations by allowing for better movement around the building. Maintaining well-defined pathways through the landscaped area would support effective responder navigation and staging, ensuring that they can carry out their duties efficiently and safely.

wildfire.recommendations: Improving defensible space around the property could be achieved by maintaining greater separation between vegetation and the structure, particularly where brush and vegetation are adjacent. Reducing the continuity of scattered trees and shrubs, as well as managing the vegetation close to the fence and structure, may help decrease potential pathways for fire spread. Utilizing the open terrain to expand defensible space may also be effective, enhancing the capability to manage fire advancement and provide safer areas for firefighters to operate. Consistent management of these areas around the residence could support long-term wildfire resilience and ensure a more prepared and fire-resilient property.
